run_focus: Day-to-day lab workflows with heavy screenshot scrutiny. Emphasize inventory placement (bottles on lab benches, aliquot tubes in freezer boxes with grid positions), creating things, renaming, moving between locations, linking objects across pages, reading/printing protocols, lab notebook entries with photos, and marking items as needing reorder. Every action must be screenshotted before AND after, and every screenshot must be Read and visually evaluated for correctness, clarity, and consistency.

personas.name: Vianney Ahn
personas.focus: DNA extraction, sample prep, pistachio pangenome
personas.workflow: notebook-heavy, daily entries, gel images, sample tracking, aliquoting into freezer boxes
tasks: Create daily notebook entry with today's extraction results (3+ samples, NanoDrop values)
Upload gel image from today's run, annotate lanes with sample names
Add 3 new sample tubes and place each in a specific freezer box grid cell
Check inventory for DNeasy kits, mark as need-more if low
Search protocols for 'PCR' and follow the genotyping protocol end-to-end
Store extracted DNA aliquots in -80 freezer box, record grid positions (A1, A2, B1)
Edit yesterday's notebook entry to add forgotten results
Cross-reference a protocol from within a notebook entry using [[wikilinks]]
Search for a reagent she knows exists but can't remember the exact name
Reproduce a batch of 10 aliquot tubes from a parent sample, place them in a new freezer box

personas.name: Alex Chen
personas.workflow: protocol creation, inventory management, project pages, equipment tracking
tasks: Create a detailed library prep protocol with 15+ steps + callouts + images
Duplicate an existing protocol and modify buffer volumes
Rename a protocol to follow the lab naming convention
Create a new project page for a grant and fill in all sections with wikilinks
Add 5 inventory items for a new kit that just arrived, place each on the right shelf
Make a new bottle of ethanol (concept -> bottle instance) and place on a lab bench
Create a new freezer box, place it on a specific shelf, add 5 tubes into it
Edit an existing wiki page to add safety information
Create a wiki page for a new piece of equipment with exact location details
Use the locations picker to move 3 items from one shelf to another

personas.name: Kayla Torres
personas.workflow: follows existing protocols, checks inventory, simple notebook entries with photos
tasks: Find the seed sterilization protocol and read it end-to-end
Print a protocol (click print, verify print preview renders cleanly, no clipped images)
Check if we have enough MS media in inventory, mark need-more if low
Create a simple notebook entry with a photo of today's plants
Search for 'ethanol' across the whole site (inventory, wiki, protocols)
Look at the lab map / location tree to find growth chamber supplies
Navigate from dashboard to protocols to notebooks (full tour)
Use mobile viewport to check protocols and create a notebook entry on her phone
Add a waste container entry for used bleach solution
Follow a wikilink from a protocol to a reagent, then from reagent to its bottle, then to the location where the bottle lives

personas.name: Dr. Monroe
personas.focus: oversight, bulletin board, dashboard review, project status
personas.workflow: dashboard -> check updates -> review notebooks -> check inventory needs -> bulletin
tasks: Review dashboard: are stats correct? Recent updates make sense? Badges showing?
Edit the bulletin board with new lab meeting time and attachments
Check all project pages -- do they have content? Are wikilinks resolving?
Review a student's notebook entry end-to-end -- is it complete? Images rendering?
Check the knowledge graph -- are connections visible? Can you filter by type?
Look at inventory needs-ordering list -- is it accurate? Toggle the flag on an item
Navigate to sample tracker, filter by status, check counts match frontmatter
Open the calendar and add a new lab meeting block
Print a protocol (verify print preview, pagination, images don't break)
Search for a specific reagent by name, then navigate to its physical bottle, then to the bottle's location

behavior_modifiers.name: rushed
behavior_modifiers.description: Types fast, doesn't wait for saves, clicks away quickly. Tests race conditions and unsaved state.

behavior_modifiers.name: careful
behavior_modifiers.description: Reads every page, verifies rendered output after every save, checks content via GitHub API. Catches stale render bugs.

behavior_modifiers.name: confused
behavior_modifiers.description: Clicks wrong buttons, uses search with typos, tries to save without required fields. Tests error handling.

behavior_modifiers.name: thorough
behavior_modifiers.description: Fills every optional field, uploads multiple images, creates deeply nested content with many wikilinks. Tests limits.

behavior_modifiers.name: mobile
behavior_modifiers.description: Uses 375x812 viewport throughout. Tests responsive layout, touch targets, bottom nav, image upload on mobile.

behavior_modifiers.name: adversarial
behavior_modifiers.description: HTML/script injection, 500+ char strings, emoji-heavy names, Unicode edge cases. Tests input sanitization.

behavior_modifiers.name: impatient
behavior_modifiers.description: Double-clicks save, refreshes mid-save, back-button after submit. Tests interruption handling.

behavior_modifiers.name: explorer
behavior_modifiers.description: URL manipulation (?doc=nonexistent), opens pages in unexpected order, deep-links to deleted items. Tests error states.

behavior_modifiers.name: ui_critic
behavior_modifiers.description: Focuses on visual coherence -- does the pill color match the type? Is the breadcrumb correct? Is the grid cell rendering the right child? Is spacing consistent? Screenshot-heavy.

behavior_modifiers.name: cross_linker
behavior_modifiers.description: Obsessed with links -- follows every wikilink, verifies bidirectional backlinks, checks that object pills render with correct icon+title, tests [[ autocomplete everywhere.

scenario_cards: Ethanol bottle day: Inventory -> Add new bottle of Ethanol Absolute (from ethanol-absolute concept). Give it a lot number, expiration, assign location to lab-bench. Save. Verify it appears in the concept's 'physical bottles' list. Open the lab bench location -- verify the new bottle is a child. Screenshot every step.

Aliquot day: Take an existing sample (e.g. sample-pistachio-4). Create 6 new tube instances named tube-pistachio-4-aliquot-1 through -6, each pointing to the sample via of:. Pick an existing box OR create a new box box-pistachio-aliquots (grid 9x9) on shelf-minus80-a-1. Assign each tube to grid positions A1 through A6. Save. Open box popup, confirm all 6 aliquots render in the grid.

New freezer box day: Create new box 'Library Prep Kit Storage' (grid 10x10) on an existing shelf. Add 3 tubes into it at specific positions. Verify the box appears in the shelf children list, the tubes appear in the box grid, and each tube shows the right breadcrumb.
Reorder day: Scan inventory, pick 3 items that are actually low, toggle need-more on each. Verify dashboard needs-ordering count updates. Toggle one back off and verify it decrements.

Lab notebook full day: Create a new notebook entry under your own folder. Add a title, body with 3 section headers, a table of 4 columns x 6 rows, a callout, a wikilink to an existing protocol, upload 2 photos (plant growth, gel), annotate one photo with a label. Save. Reload. Verify all content renders correctly.

Print protocol day: Open a protocol with images. Click print. Verify the print preview opens, images load fully (not blank), pagination doesn't clip figures, headings stay with their sections. Screenshot the print preview.
Rename + relink day: Rename an existing protocol. Verify any wikilinks pointing to the old slug still work (redirects or auto-updated). Check backlinks in the connections panel.

Equipment move day: Pick 3 inventory items currently on shelf A. Use the locations picker to move all 3 to shelf B. Verify the tree view updates on lab-map page. Verify the old shelf no longer lists them as children.

New user onboarding: Create a new person card (frontmatter type=person, name, role, focus). Create a notebook folder for them. Create their first entry. Verify the person appears on the People page and their notebook sorts correctly.

Concept+instance linking: Pick a reagent concept with zero bottles. Create 2 physical bottle instances. Verify the concept's popup column 3 now lists both bottles. Verify inventory table hides the individual bottles under the concept row.

Cross-page wikilink crawl: From the dashboard, follow a link to a protocol. From the protocol, follow a wikilink to a reagent. From the reagent to a bottle. From the bottle to a location. Each step: screenshot + evaluate breadcrumb + verify page loads correctly.
Photo-heavy notebook: Upload 5 images to one notebook entry. Annotate 3 (boxes + labels). Resize 2 smaller. Save. Reload. Verify all 5 render at the right sizes, annotations visible, no broken paths.
Mobile lab notebook: On mobile viewport, create a notebook entry with a photo + text. Verify the Aa mobile toolbar works, the fab button layout, the image upload from mobile camera input.
Bulk sample tracker: Add 10 samples in rapid succession with varying status. Filter by status. Verify counts. Check a random sample's full detail page.
Search every surface: Global search for an obscure term (e.g. 'DNeasy' or a rare reagent name). Verify it returns hits across inventory, protocols, notebooks, wiki with correct context snippets.
Backlinks sanity: Pick any object with known inbound references (e.g. a sample referenced by tubes). Open its popup. Verify all backlinks listed, each one clickable, each destination loads.
Calendar add/delete: Add a new lab block at a specific hour. Verify it appears in the week grid. Delete it. Verify it disappears.
Waste container: Create a new waste container for ethanol-contaminated gloves. Verify days-held starts at 0 and updates on reload.

screenshot_protocol.rule_1: Every click, save, navigation, or form submit MUST have a before AND after screenshot.
screenshot_protocol.rule_2: Save all cycle screenshots under /tmp/qa-screenshots/cycleN/ (create the dir).
screenshot_protocol.rule_3: Name screenshots descriptively: cycleN-stepM-<persona>-<action>.png e.g. cycle9-03-vianney-aliquot-box-popup.png
screenshot_protocol.rule_4: After taking each screenshot, READ it with the Read tool. Do NOT skip. Evaluate: is the UI rendering correctly? Is the right object loaded? Are pills colored right? Are icons right? Is the breadcrumb correct? Is there overflow, clipping, or missing text?
screenshot_protocol.rule_5: Write a visual evaluation string per screenshot into the cycle's workflows_tested entry under screenshots: [{path, what_it_shows, evaluation, issues_found}].
screenshot_protocol.rule_6: If a screenshot reveals a visual issue (even a cosmetic one), log it as a bug with severity: visual. Attempt a fix if in scope (app/ or docs/ only).

workflows_tested.task: Created new 9x9 box-pistachio-aliquots on shelf-minus80-a-1, then 6 aliquot tubes (A1-A6) referencing sample-pistachio-4 via wikilinks. Verified breadcrumbs, grid placement, hierarchy tree, and backlinks from sample popup.

screenshots.shows: Lab Map page with Locations tree expanded to room/freezer/shelf level
screenshots.evaluation: Tree renders cleanly. Robbins Hall 0170 expanded; -80C Freezer A shows 3 items; Shelf 1 (2 items), Shelf 2 (2 items), Shelf 3, 4C Fridge (14 items).
issues_found: Top nav 'Lab Map' label is truncated to 'Lab M' under the user menu — minor visual issue but consistent across all screenshots
The 'Map design in progress' SVG placeholder has overlapping text where 'Map design' overlays 'Bench 1' and 'in progress' overlays 'Bench 2' — looks awkward

screenshots.shows: Pistachio Tree #4 sample popup with fields panel, body, and 3 references in col 3
screenshots.evaluation: Three-column layout renders correctly. Fields show Name/Sample ID/Species/Project/Lead/Sequencing Type/Status. Body has correctly-colored wikilink pills (Pistachio Leaf 1/2 in teal=Tube, Pistachio Tree #4 in purple=Sample). Right column lists 3 REFERENCES (DNA Extract 1, Leaf 1, Leaf 2).

screenshots.shows: Shelf 1 popup with breadcrumb and contents (Box A, Pistachio DNA Box)
screenshots.evaluation: Breadcrumb chain Robbins Hall 0170 / -80C Freezer A / Shelf 1 renders correctly with type-colored pills. Contents pane lists 2 child boxes plus Add item button. Fields panel shows Type=Shelf, Parent=-80C Freezer A.

screenshots.shows: Lab map after creating new box; Shelf 1 child count incremented from 2 to 3 items
screenshots.evaluation: patchObjectIndex correctly updates the tree counter without a deploy. Tree still has the same items expanded.

screenshots.shows: New empty 9x9 box popup with 81 dashed empty cells labeled A1-I9
screenshots.evaluation: Grid renders correctly with column headers 1-9 and row headers A-I. All 81 cells dashed (empty state). Header reads '9 × 9 · 0 items'. Body wikilinks render correctly: Pistachio Tree #4 (purple Sample pill), Shelf 1 (grey-blue Shelf pill).
issues_found: Breadcrumb wraps to two lines and ends with a dangling trailing slash ('Pistachio Aliquots Box /' with nothing after). Cosmetic but odd.

screenshots.shows: Same box popup after creating 6 tubes; row A cells 1-6 occupied (teal), 7-9 empty
screenshots.evaluation: Header now reads '9 × 9 · 6 items'. Row A shows 6 teal-filled cells with 'P4-' label fragments visible. Cells A7-A9 stay empty. Other rows untouched. Grid correctly reflects new children via patched object index.
issues_found: Cell labels are truncated to 'P4-' — the second line of label_2 ('4/21') is hidden. Acceptable given the small cell size, but worth noting for label-design choices.

screenshots.shows: Tube popup with full 5-level breadcrumb chain
screenshots.evaluation: Breadcrumb chain wraps cleanly: Robbins Hall 0170 / -80C Freezer A / Shelf 1 / Pistachio Aliquots Box / Pistachio 4 Aliquot 3. All pill colors match type system. Position field shows A3, label_2 shows 'P4-A3 / 4/21'. Body wikilinks render correctly.

screenshots.shows: Sample popup AFTER fix: now shows 10 REFERENCES instead of 3 — all 6 new aliquots + the new box + the 3 original tubes
screenshots.evaluation: Fix verified. patchLinkIndex correctly mirrors the body's outbound wikilinks into a localStorage overlay so backlinks appear immediately without waiting for a redeploy. References now: Aliquot 1-6 (Tube), Pistachio Aliquots Box (Box), DNA Extract 1, Leaf 1, Leaf 2.

screenshots.shows: Shelf 1 popup now lists 3 children including the new Pistachio Aliquots Box
screenshots.evaluation: Parent->children relationship via the patched object index works correctly. Box A, Pistachio Aliquots Box, Pistachio DNA Box all listed.

screenshots.shows: Filtered lab-map tree showing the full hierarchy from Robbins Hall down to all 6 aliquot tubes with grid positions
screenshots.evaluation: Showcase view: tree filter 'aliquot' walks down Room -> Freezer -> Shelf -> Box -> 6 Tubes, each tube row shows its grid position (A1-A6) on the right. Box shows '9x9' grid label and '6 items' count.

bugs.summary: Backlinks panel did not include newly-created files until the next site deploy
bugs.evidence: step10 screenshot from initial run showed only 3 REFERENCES on sample-pistachio-4 popup despite 6 new tubes wikilinking to it
bugs.root_cause: fetchLinkIndex() loaded the static link-index.json built by scripts/build-object-index.py and had no localStorage patch layer like fetchObjectIndex did

fixes: Added patchLinkIndex / removeFromLinkIndex / extractWikilinkTargets in app/js/github-api.js with a localStorage overlay applied in fetchLinkIndex; wired patchLinkIndex into the editor save flow in app/js/editor-modal.js
observations: 5-level breadcrumb (Room/Freezer/Shelf/Box/Tube) renders cleanly with type-colored pills
Object-index patches cascade correctly to tree counters and Contents lists without redeploy
Type system colors are consistent across pills, popup headers, and grid cells (Box brown, Tube teal, Shelf grey-blue, Freezer light-blue, Sample purple, Room dark)
Cosmetic issue: breadcrumb wraps to two lines for deep chains and leaves a trailing slash with no following pill
Cosmetic issue: top nav 'Lab Map' is truncated to 'Lab M' (likely a label-width issue with the user menu)
Cosmetic issue: Map design placeholder has overlapping text labels

workflows_tested.task: Created a new bottle instance (bottle-ethanol-absolute-qa2-*) pointing at resources/ethanol-absolute via `of:`, placed it on Chemistry Bench Area. Verified concept popup instance count incremented from 4 to 5. Moved the bottle to cabinet-flammable, verified the cabinet's Contents pane picked up the bottle post-fix.

screenshots.shows: Ethanol Absolute concept popup with updated instances column 3 (5 bottles including new QA2 instance)
screenshots.evaluation: Concept popup renders three-column layout: fields (kogen, Chemical), body (description with inventory:// link), CONTENTS column now includes the new bottle. patchLinkIndex immediately reflected the new wikilink target.

screenshots.shows: New bottle popup at creation time — fields show Type=Bottle, Of=resources/ethanol-absolute, Parent=Chemistry Bench Area, Lot/Expiration
screenshots.evaluation: Popup fields render correctly. BUT: no breadcrumb pills at top of body (unlike the well-formed breadcrumb seen in cycle 1 tube popups). Contents panel says 'No references to this object yet.' which is expected for a leaf object.
issues_found: BREADCRUMB-MISSING-ON-NEW-OBJECT: freshly created bottle popup shows no breadcrumb pills even though parent is set. Root cause: hierarchy cache stale after save.

screenshots.shows: Flammable Cabinet popup after the bottle was moved from bench to cabinet; CONTENTS lists many bottles (1-Octanol, 3-Methylbutanol, Ethanol Absolute, Ethyl Alcohol Absolute, etc.)
screenshots.evaluation: Cabinet popup renders correctly with breadcrumb Robbins Hall 0170 / Flammable Cabinet, Type=Container pill, fields panel, and a scrollable CONTENTS list of 16+ bottles.
issues_found: Initial run (pre-fix) did NOT show the moved bottle in the contents list because the hierarchy cache was not invalidated after patchObjectIndex. Post-fix verification (cycle2-verify/03-cabinet-after-move.png) confirms the moved bottle appears as expected.

screenshots.shows: Verify run of Flammable Cabinet popup after moving a test bottle, CONTENTS includes 'Ethanol Absolute (verify)' entry
screenshots.evaluation: Fix verified. Cabinet contents pane correctly lists the newly-moved bottle immediately after the save flow completes, no deploy required. Hierarchy cache invalidation works.

screenshots.shows: Lab Map tree filtered by 'qa2' showing the bottle under its new cabinet parent
screenshots.evaluation: Filter walks down the hierarchy from the room to the cabinet to the specific bottle. Tree reflects the patched-index state correctly.

bugs.summary: Children lists and breadcrumbs rendered from Lab.hierarchy._graph went stale after a save via patchObjectIndex — a new bottle/tube/box did not appear in its parent's CONTENTS until full reload and the child's own popup had no breadcrumb
bugs.evidence: step07 bench popup did not list the new bottle; step08 bottle popup missing breadcrumb; step15 cabinet-after-move did not list the bottle (log flag hasBottle=false)
bugs.root_cause: Lab.hierarchy built a derived cache (children map + parent chain) from the index once and never invalidated it when patchObjectIndex / removeFromObjectIndex mutated the underlying store.

fixes: Invalidate Lab.hierarchy cache from patchObjectIndex / removeFromObjectIndex so children-lists and breadcrumbs reflect newly-saved objects immediately (app/js/github-api.js, commit 3d94ff8)
observations: Concept -> instance relationship works: a new bottle with `of: resources/ethanol-absolute` shows up in the concept's CONTENTS/backlinks immediately via patchLinkIndex from cycle 1.
Parent relationship previously lagged until hierarchy cache was invalidated — fixed in cycle 2.
Move-between-parents (bench -> cabinet) works by saving the file with the new `parent:` frontmatter; both the old and new parents reflect the change after the cache-invalidation fix.
Inventory page renders the new bottle as a row in the ethanol-grouped section; concept grouping hides individual bottles under the concept pill.

workflows_tested.task: Toggled 3 in_stock inventory items (1,8-Naphthalic anhydride, 1M Tris-HCL, Analytical Balance) to needs_more via the status pill (cycleStatus), verified inventory stat cards update correctly, checked dashboard Inventory Status, then cycled one item back through out_of_stock → in_stock to verify decrement. Cleanup via direct API for remaining 2 items.

screenshots.shows: Dashboard baseline — Inventory Status card shows 226 In Stock / 1 Out of Stock / 1 alert row (Native Barcoding Kit 24 V14, Out of Stock)
screenshots.evaluation: Card header 'INVENTORY STATUS' uppercase small caps with orange shopping_cart icon. Counts row compact with icons + numbers + labels inline. Single alert row has red cancel icon + item title + 'Out of Stock' red pill.

screenshots.shows: Inventory page baseline — 4 stat cards (250 TOTAL / 228 IN STOCK / 0 NEEDS MORE / 1 OUT OF STOCK) + scrollable table with name/type/status/qty/location columns
screenshots.evaluation: Stat card layout clean. Status column shows green 'In Stock' pills for all visible rows. Table sorted by name. Typography consistent. Search + filter bar visible at top.

screenshots.shows: Inventory page after 3 cycleStatus toggles — stat cards now 250/225/3/1; rows for 1,8-Naphthalic anhydride and 1M Tris-HCL show amber 'Needs More' pills with left amber border highlight; green toast at bottom right: 'Analytical Balance: Needs More'
screenshots.evaluation: Stat counts update in real time as each cycleStatus call completes. Amber row highlight (4px left border + light amber background) is a tasteful alert treatment. Toast appears in bottom-right with correct status label. No layout shift.

screenshots.shows: Inventory page filtered to needs_more — table collapses to the 3 toggled rows (1,8-Naphthalic anhydride / 1M Tris-HCL / Analytical Balance), each with amber Needs More pill and amber left-border highlight
screenshots.evaluation: Filter dropdown shows 'Needs More'. Only matching rows remain. Stat cards unchanged (they show totals, not filtered counts). Amber styling consistent across all three alert rows.

screenshots.shows: Dashboard after 3 toggles (BEFORE FIX) — STILL shows 226 In Stock / 1 Out of Stock / 1 alert, identical to baseline. None of the 3 toggled items appear in the alert list.
screenshots.evaluation: BUG: dashboard did not reflect the 3 toggled items. Counts did not change, alerts list is stale. Cause: dashboard.html init() called raw `fetch(BASE + 'object-index.json')` rather than Lab.gh.fetchObjectIndex, bypassing the localStorage patch overlay.
issues_found: STALE-DASHBOARD-INDEX functional bug — dashboard stats + alerts out of sync with inventory page after status toggles

screenshots.shows: Inventory after 2 additional toggles on pick[0] (needs_more → out_of_stock → in_stock): stats now 250/226/2/1; 1,8-Naphthalic anhydride row back to green In Stock; 1M Tris-HCL still amber; green toast '1,8-Naphthalic anhydride: In Stock'
screenshots.evaluation: STATUS_CYCLE traversal works correctly — three consecutive cycleStatus calls move item through in_stock → needs_more → out_of_stock → in_stock. Stat cards decrement immediately. Pill color matches status.

screenshots.shows: POST-FIX verification — Dashboard Inventory Status now shows 224 In Stock / 2 Needs More / 1 Out of Stock and the alert list includes the 2 toggled items (1,8-Naphthalic anhydride + 1-HYDROXY-3-(3-TRIFLUOROMETHYLPHENYL)UREA, both with amber Needs More pills) plus the pre-existing Native Barcoding Kit (red Out of Stock)
screenshots.evaluation: FIX CONFIRMED. Dashboard now applies the localStorage patch overlay via Lab.gh.fetchObjectIndex. Counts match expected values. Alert list correctly includes newly-toggled items with correct amber/red badges and material icons (warning_amber triangle, cancel X). Recent Updates column on the right shows the cycle-3 cleanup commits. Layout and typography consistent with the rest of the dashboard.

bugs.summary: Dashboard Inventory Status card showed stale counts and alert list after inventory items had their status toggled on the inventory page
bugs.evidence: step09-after-dashboard-after-3-toggles.png is identical to step01 baseline despite 3 items being toggled to needs_more; programmatic check returned picksFound:[false,false,false] and alertCount unchanged
bugs.root_cause: dashboard.html init() called `fetch(BASE + 'object-index.json')` directly instead of going through Lab.gh.fetchObjectIndex, which applies the localStorage patch overlay populated by patchObjectIndex. The static object-index.json is only rebuilt on deploy, so between an inventory edit and the next deploy the dashboard rendered stale data.

fixes: dashboard.html init() now prefers Lab.gh.fetchObjectIndex (with its localStorage overlay) and falls back to raw fetch only if Lab.gh is unavailable (commit 763ad51)
observations: cycleStatus correctly walks the STATUS_CYCLE ring (in_stock → needs_more → out_of_stock → in_stock) and commits each step to GitHub.
Inventory page stat cards (In Stock / Needs More / Out of Stock) are immediately correct after each toggle — they read from the in-memory inventory array updated by cycleStatus.
Alert rows on the inventory table get a 4px amber/red left border and a light tint — consistent with the status pill color.
The Inventory Status card on the dashboard is fed from the same object-index, so once the overlay was applied the alert list auto-populated with no other changes.

Dashboard top-stat 'INVENTORY 480' is the raw total of *all* stock+resource objects (including externals), whereas the status counts (224/2/1) only sum tracked statuses — so they don't add up to 480. That is intentional but could confuse a lab member.
Verification file: tests/qa-cycle-3-verify.mjs toggles 2 items, navigates to dashboard, asserts picksFound:[true,true] — kept in the repo as a regression harness for the fix.

screenshots.shows: Bottle popup (stocks/bottle-ethanol-absolute) with breadcrumb Robbins Hall 0170 / Flammable Cabinet / Ethanol Absolute and fields: Type=Bottle, Link, Name, Of (concept slug)=resources/ethanol-absolute (PLAIN TEXT), Parent=Flammable Cabinet (clickable pill), Quantity=1, Unit=each
screenshots.evaluation: BUG FOUND: 'Of (concept slug)' field rendered as plain text 'resources/ethanol-absolute' while 'Parent' is a clickable pill. Inconsistent — cross_linker cannot navigate bottle → concept from the fields column. Breadcrumb renders correctly with 3-pill chain.
issues_found: OF-FIELD-NOT-CLICKABLE (functional, fixed): `of` field in bottle popup was plain text; should mirror `parent` field which is a clickable object pill

screenshots.shows: Flammable Cabinet popup with breadcrumb Robbins Hall 0170 / Flammable Cabinet, fields (Type=Container, Link, Name, Parent=Robbins Hall 0170 pill, Label 1=Flammable Cabinet), body text about auto-migration, and CONTENTS list of 16 bottles (1-Octanol, 3-Methylbutanol, Aceto-Orcein Solution 2%, Acetone, Activated charcoal, Dimethyl sulfoxide, Ethanol Absolute, Ethyl Alcohol Absolute, Glacial Acetic Acid, Glacial Acetic Acid, Isopropyl alcohol, Isopropyl alcohol, Isopropyl alcohol 70% in water, Isopropyl alcohol 70% in water, Methanol, Reagent Alcohol 100%)
screenshots.evaluation: Parent→children hierarchy correct (cabinet is child of room, 16 bottles are children of cabinet). Parent pill is clickable. But contents rows lack a subtitle — reagent popup showed '1 each · cabinet flammable' secondary line per row; cabinet popup shows only the title. Two 'Glacial Acetic Acid' rows and two 'Isopropyl alcohol' rows render identically without differentiator.
issues_found: DUPLICATE-TITLE-IN-CONTENTS (cosmetic, open): cabinet Contents list renders objects with identical titles identically — no lot / slug / expiration subtitle to disambiguate

screenshots.shows: Robbins Hall 0170 room popup with single-pill breadcrumb (Robbins Hall 0170), fields (Type=Room, Link, Name, Label 1 (full)=Monroe Lab), body describing the wet lab, and CONTENTS list of 9 children (-80C Freezer A, 4C Fridge (Main), Bench, Chemical Cabinet, Chemistry Bench Area, Corrosive Cabinet, Flammable Cabinet, Freezer (-20C), Fume hood bay)
screenshots.evaluation: Navigation from cabinet→room via parent pill works. Contents column correctly lists all 9 direct children of the room, each with its type icon. Single-pill breadcrumb is arguably redundant with the big title right below it.
issues_found: ROOM-BREADCRUMB-REDUNDANT (cosmetic, minor): for top-of-hierarchy rooms the breadcrumb shows just the self pill, duplicating the H1 title

screenshots.shows: Lab Map tree filtered to 'ethanol': Robbins Hall 0170 → Bench (Ethanol 70% ×2) + Chemical Cabinet (Formaldehyde match via 'methanol') + Corrosive Cabinet (2-Mercaptoethanol) + Flammable Cabinet (Ethanol Absolute, Methanol)
screenshots.evaluation: Filter does substring-match against titles, which surfaces 'methanol' / '2-mercaptoethanol' / 'formaldehyde (16% methanol free)' as false-positives for an 'ethanol' search. Acceptable but not word-boundary. Tree expansion + highlighted result rows render with light teal background.
issues_found: FILTER-SUBSTRING-ONLY (minor): the lab-map filter uses substring match, so 'ethanol' matches 'methanol'

screenshots.shows: Inventory page with 4 stat cards (250/228/0/1), filter row, and full inventory table — Name, Type, Status, Qty, Location, Updated, Actions columns
screenshots.evaluation: Layout clean. Type and Status pills colored consistently. BUT: the blue issue-reporter FAB at bottom-left covers the first column of the table row at y≈797 — the 'Agar (non-TC)' row displays as 'r (non-TC)' because 'Aga' is hidden behind the FAB.
issues_found: FAB-OVERLAPS-TABLE (visual, open): issue-reporter FAB at fixed bottom-left overlaps the first data column of long tables (inventory, waste) making row text partially unreadable

screenshots.shows: POST-FIX bottle popup — 'Of (concept slug)' field now shows a clickable teal Reagent pill 'Ethanol Absolute' (flask icon). Parent pill still renders Flammable Cabinet. Both fields are now symmetric: clickable object pills.
screenshots.evaluation: Fix verified visually. The `of` field placeholder `<span data-of-pill>` is upgraded to a real object pill with the concept's type color + icon + click handler, identical to how the parent field has always worked.

screenshots.shows: After clicking the new Of pill — reagent popup for Ethanol Absolute opens (title matches, contents column lists the bottle instance + 3 protocol references).
screenshots.evaluation: Navigation works end-to-end: bottle popup → click Of pill → concept popup. Round-trip confirmed cross_linker-complete.

bugs.summary: `of` field in instance popups (bottle/tube) rendered as plain slug text, not as a clickable object pill — unlike `parent` which has always rendered as a pill.
bugs.evidence: screenshots/cycle4/07-bottle-popup-opened.png shows 'resources/ethanol-absolute' as raw grey text in the Of field row while the Parent row on the same popup shows a clickable 'Flammable Cabinet' pill
bugs.root_cause: renderFields() in app/js/editor-modal.js had a special case for `field.key === 'parent'` that emitted a <span data-parent-pill> placeholder for upgradeParentField() to replace with a proper pill. No equivalent case existed for `field.key === 'of'`, so it fell through to the generic text renderer.

fixes: Added an 'of' field branch in renderFields that emits a <span data-of-pill> placeholder parallel to the parent-pill placeholder, and extended upgradeParentField to also upgrade `[data-of-pill]` placeholders (app/js/editor-modal.js, commit b0da836)
observations: Four-step wikilink crawl (protocol → reagent → bottle → cabinet → room) works end-to-end via the editor-modal popup stack. Escape walks the nav stack back.
Object pills render with correct type color + icon at every level (Reagent=purple flask, Bottle=orange wine-bottle, Container=grey-blue cube, Room=grey-blue door).
Breadcrumb chain is rendered at the top of the body column as type-colored pills; shows full ancestor chain + self for non-root objects, just self for rooms.
Parent→children relationship is correctly reflected in the Contents column at every level — no caching lag (cache invalidation fix from cycle 2 is holding).
Instance→concept relationship works via CONTENTS column (`1 INSTANCE IN LAB` section on the concept popup) and now ALSO via the Of pill in the bottle popup's fields column after the cycle-4 fix.
Contents rows for siblings with identical titles (e.g., two `Glacial Acetic Acid` bottles) are indistinguishable in the cabinet popup — no lot/slug/expiration subtitle.
The issue-reporter FAB at fixed bottom-left overlaps the first data column of wide tables (inventory) — a row at y≈797 has its first ~40px hidden behind the blue button.
Lab-map filter uses case-insensitive substring match; 'ethanol' matches 'methanol' and '2-mercaptoethanol'.

bugs_found: BACKLINKS-LAG (functional, fixed in cycle 1): newly-created or freshly-edited files were missing from the backlinks panel of every wikilink target until the next deploy

HIERARCHY-CACHE-STALE (functional, fixed in cycle 2): after a save the Lab.hierarchy cache retained its pre-save snapshot, so parent CONTENTS + child breadcrumbs were missing for new objects until reload

STALE-DASHBOARD-INDEX (functional, fixed in cycle 3): dashboard Inventory Status card did raw fetch of object-index.json, bypassing the localStorage patch overlay — counts + alerts lagged until site redeploy

OF-FIELD-NOT-CLICKABLE (functional, fixed in cycle 4): the `of` field on bottle/tube popups rendered as plain slug text while the `parent` field was a clickable object pill — cross_linker could not navigate instance → concept from the fields column
NAV-TRUNCATION (cosmetic, open): top nav 'Lab Map' tab label appears truncated to 'Lab M' on 1440-wide viewport
MAP-PLACEHOLDER-OVERLAP (cosmetic, open): SVG placeholder for the floor-plan has overlapping text labels
BREADCRUMB-DANGLING-SLASH (cosmetic, open): popup breadcrumb wraps to two lines and leaves a trailing '/' with nothing after on deep chains

DASHBOARD-TOTAL-VS-STATUS-MISMATCH (cosmetic/ux, open): dashboard top card 'INVENTORY 480' counts all resource+stock objects while the Inventory Status counts (e.g. 226/0/1 = 227) only sum tracked statuses — the two numbers don't relate, which may confuse lab members

DUPLICATE-TITLE-IN-CONTENTS (cosmetic, open): contents column of a location popup lists children with identical titles identically — two `Glacial Acetic Acid` bottles, two `Isopropyl alcohol` bottles, etc. render the same with no lot/slug/expiration subtitle to disambiguate
FAB-OVERLAPS-TABLE (visual, open): fixed-position issue-reporter FAB at bottom-left covers the first data column of long tables (inventory, waste) at y≈797, making row text partially unreadable
ROOM-BREADCRUMB-REDUNDANT (cosmetic, minor): for root locations (rooms) the breadcrumb shows only the self-pill, duplicating the H1 title right below
FILTER-SUBSTRING-ONLY (minor): lab-map filter matches substrings without word boundaries — searching 'ethanol' matches 'methanol' and '2-mercaptoethanol'
fixes_applied: Live link-index patching for instant backlinks (app/js/github-api.js + app/js/editor-modal.js, commit 642579c)
Hierarchy cache invalidation on patchObjectIndex / removeFromObjectIndex (app/js/github-api.js, commit 3d94ff8)
Dashboard uses Lab.gh.fetchObjectIndex so the localStorage patch overlay flows through (app/dashboard.html, commit 763ad51)
`of` field renders a clickable object pill matching the `parent` field treatment — new data-of-pill placeholder + extended upgradeParentField (app/js/editor-modal.js, commit b0da836)
improvements_proposed: Truncate label_2 only to its first line for grid-cell display so 'P4-A1' shows without losing the date line, OR shrink the date to a corner badge
Either omit the trailing breadcrumb slash on wrapped lines or include the current item as a final pill on the next line
screenshots_taken: /tmp/qa-screenshots/cycle3/ — 27 screenshots + _log.json, every status toggle + dashboard/inventory state captured before/after
/tmp/qa-screenshots/cycle3-verify/ — 3 verification screenshots confirming the dashboard fix
/tmp/qa-screenshots/cycle4/ — 21 screenshots + _log.json, full wikilink crawl with captured popup states at each level
/tmp/qa-screenshots/cycle4-verify/ — 2 verification screenshots confirming the `of` pill upgrade
notes: Fresh run started 2026-04-21. Prior 8-cycle run archived to tests/qa-state.archive-2026-04-12.json.
Screenshot discipline is the PRIMARY success criterion this run -- take many, READ each one, evaluate visually, log visual issues.

Cycle 1: Vianney + ui_critic + Aliquot day. Created box-pistachio-aliquots-* (9x9) on shelf-minus80-a-1, then 6 tubes at A1-A6. All artifacts cleaned up at end of cycle. Backlinks bug found and fixed; deployed; re-verified.

Cycle 2: Alex Chen + cross_linker + Ethanol bottle day. Created bottle-ethanol-absolute-qa2-* pointing to the ethanol-absolute concept, placed on Chemistry Bench Area, then moved to Flammable Cabinet. All artifacts cleaned up. Hierarchy cache bug found and fixed (children + breadcrumbs now refresh immediately after save).

Cycle 3: Dr. Monroe + ui_critic + Reorder day. Toggled 3 items to needs_more, verified inventory stat cards and dashboard alert list. Caught a stale-dashboard-index bug (dashboard raw-fetched object-index.json, bypassing patch overlay). Fixed in commit 763ad51, verified via tests/qa-cycle-3-verify.mjs (kept as regression harness). All items restored to in_stock.

Cycle 4: Kayla Torres (undergrad) + cross_linker + Cross-page wikilink crawl. Read-only walk: Dashboard → QIAGEN DNeasy protocol → Ethanol Absolute reagent (popup) → ethanol-absolute bottle (popup with breadcrumb) → Flammable Cabinet (cabinet popup, 16 bottles) → Robbins Hall 0170 (room popup, 9 children). Found OF-FIELD-NOT-CLICKABLE bug: the `of` field on a bottle popup was plain text while `parent` was a clickable pill. Fix (commit b0da836) adds a parallel `data-of-pill` placeholder and extends upgradeParentField to handle both. Verified via tests/qa-cycle-4-verify.mjs — kept as regression harness.
